title: Autonomy-level trade-off (AL0-AL5)
text: trade KIA reduction versus IHL compliance. default AL scenario mapping requires full MEDTACS-SIM sweep; demonstrate two AL points shipped example data.
code: al <- al_efficiency(   sim,   al_scenarios = c("0" = "M-S00", "1" = "M-S01"),   ihl_threshold = 0.80,   n_bootstrap   = 200 ) al$tradeoff_table #> # A tibble: 2 × 10 #>      al scenario kia_median kia_ci_lo kia_ci_hi kia_reduction_pct ihl_index #>   <int> <chr>         <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>             <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1     0 M-S00         0.341     0.328     0.353              0        0.781 #> 2     1 M-S01         0.272     0.260     0.290              6.91     0.847 #> # ℹ 3 more variables: above_threshold <lgl>, al_ratio <dbl>, n_reps <int>

title: Autonomy-level trade-off (AL0-AL5)
code: plot_al_tradeoff(al) #> `height` was translated to `width`.

title: Autonomy-level trade-off (AL0-AL5)
text: optimal_al slot holds AL level highest KIA reduction staying IHL threshold: IHL violations ():
code: al$optimal_al #> [1] 1 al$ihl_violations #> # A tibble: 1 × 11 #>      al scenario kia_median kia_ci_lo kia_ci_hi kia_reduction_pct ihl_index #>   <int> <chr>         <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>             <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1     0 M-S00         0.341     0.328     0.353                 0     0.781 #> # ℹ 4 more variables: above_threshold <lgl>, al_ratio <dbl>, n_reps <int>, #> #   ihl_deficit <dbl>

title: Doctrine effects: MUF vs. Military Necessity
text: two MEDTACS scenarios M-S07 (Military Necessity) M-S08 (Medical Urgency First) differ triage doctrine applied.
code: doc <- doctrine_effect(   sim,   muf_scenario    = "M-S08",   milnec_scenario = "M-S07",   n_bootstrap     = 500 ) print(doc) #>  #> ── dynasimR_doctrine ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • MUF: "M-S08" #> • MilNec: "M-S07" #> • n (reps): 50 #>  #> ── Delta KIA ── #>  #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   identity median_pct_points  ci_lo ci_hi #>   <chr>                <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 all                  -7.37  -9.10 -5.53 #> 2 CIVILIAN             -9.39 -16.1  -3.59 #> 3 FOE                 -11.3  -14.9  -7.65 #> 4 FRIEND              -10.6  -14.1  -7.54 #> ── Narrative ── #> Under Medical Urgency First doctrine (scenario M-S08), a KIA-rate reduction of 7.4 percentage points (95\%-CI: -9.1 to -5.5) was observed versus prioritisation of own forces (scenario M-S07) (Wilcoxon test: W = 215, p < 0.001). The IHL-Compliance Index was higher under MUF doctrine (0.919 vs. 0.658).

title: Doctrine effects: MUF vs. Military Necessity
code: plot_doctrine(doc) #> `height` was translated to `width`.

title: Doctrine effects: MUF vs. Military Necessity
text: narrative slot LaTeX-escaped string ready drop manuscript: Medical Urgency First doctrine (scenario M-S08), KIA-rate reduction 7.4 percentage points (95%-CI: -9.1 -5.5) observed versus prioritisation forces (scenario M-S07) (Wilcoxon test: W = 215, p < 0.001). IHL-Compliance Index higher MUF doctrine (0.919 vs. 0.658).
code: cat(doc$narrative)

title: Doctrine effects: MUF vs. Military Necessity
code: doc$effect_sizes #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   metric                value #>   <chr>                 <dbl> #> 1 Cohen_d_KIA         -1.95   #> 2 Risk_Difference_KIA -0.0685 #> 3 NNT_surrogate       14.6

title: Getting started with dynasimR
code: sim <- load_example_data() print(sim) #>  #> ── dynasimR_data ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Scenarios: 4 #> • Simulation: "MEDTACS" #> • Summary rows: 200 #> • Casualty events: 16000 #> • Loaded: "2026-04-17 15:25" #> • Path: /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/dynasimR/extdata

title: Getting started with dynasimR
code: km <- km_estimate(sim, endpoint = "role2", stratify_by = "scenario") plot_km(km, title = "Survival-to-Role-2") #> Warning: Removed 2 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_ribbon()`).

title: Getting started with dynasimR
code: doc <- doctrine_effect(sim,   muf_scenario    = "M-S08",   milnec_scenario = "M-S07",   n_bootstrap     = 200) cat(doc$narrative) #> Under Medical Urgency First doctrine (scenario M-S08), a KIA-rate reduction of 7.4 percentage points (95\%-CI: -9 to -5.6) was observed versus prioritisation of own forces (scenario M-S07) (Wilcoxon test: W = 215, p < 0.001). The IHL-Compliance Index was higher under MUF doctrine (0.919 vs. 0.658).

title: Getting started with dynasimR
text: two AL points present shipped example data, machinery full MEDTACS-SIM output.
code: al <- al_efficiency(   sim,   al_scenarios  = c("0" = "M-S00", "1" = "M-S01"),   ihl_threshold = 0.80,   n_bootstrap   = 200 ) plot_al_tradeoff(al) #> `height` was translated to `width`.

title: Getting started with dynasimR
code: export_latex_table(   data     = doc$effect_sizes,   filename = "doctrine_table.tex",   caption  = "Doctrine effect sizes.",   label    = "doctrine" )

title: Getting started with dynasimR
code: launch_app()                                   # example data launch_app(data_dir = "~/medtacs-sim/data/raw")

title: Manuscript export workflow
text: generated .tex uses \botrule (required sn-jnl.cls) escapes </> character cells.
code: library(dynasimR) sim <- load_example_data() doc <- doctrine_effect(sim,   muf_scenario    = "M-S08",   milnec_scenario = "M-S07")  export_latex_table(   data     = doc$effect_sizes,   filename = "table_doctrine.tex",   caption  = "Doctrine effect sizes (Cohen's d and risk difference).",   label    = "doctrine",   note     = "n = 50 replications per scenario." )

title: Manuscript export workflow
code: km <- km_estimate(sim, endpoint = "role2") export_figure(   plot      = plot_km(km),   filename  = "figure_km.pdf",   width_mm  = 174,          # Springer single column   height_mm = 110 )

title: Manuscript export workflow
text: Given source sn-article.tex placeholders like [XX_DOCTRINE_DELTA_KIA], substitute one call:
code: reps <- fill_placeholders(   sim_data          = sim,   tex_file          = "sn-article.tex",   output_file       = "sn-article_filled.tex",   muf_scenario      = "M-S08",   milnec_scenario   = "M-S07",   baseline_scenario = "M-S00" ) reps          # named character vector of substitutions performed

title: Patient flow analysis
text: Role2 (time_to_role2) present shipped example, machinery generalises Role1..Role4 columns exist simulation output.
code: rt <- role_throughput(sim) rt #> # A tibble: 4 × 7 #>   scenario role      n median_min   q25   q75 completed_frac #>   <chr>    <chr> <int>      <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1 M-S00    Role2  4000        153    77  258              NA #> 2 M-S01    Role2  4000        145    74  253              NA #> 3 M-S07    Role2  4000        151    80  259              NA #> 4 M-S08    Role2  4000        151    77  259.             NA

title: Patient flow analysis
code: detect_bottlenecks(rt, threshold = 0.75) #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>   scenario role      n median_min   q25   q75 completed_frac is_bottleneck #>   <chr>    <chr> <int>      <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>          <dbl> <lgl>         #> 1 M-S00    Role2  4000        153    77   258             NA TRUE

title: Patient flow analysis
code: plot_scenario_heatmap(   sim,   metrics = c("median_time_to_first_care",               "median_time_to_role2") )

code: @Manual{,   title = {dynasimR: Dynamic Agent-Node Simulation Analysis},   author = {Raban A. Heller},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/rabanheller/dynasimR}, }

title: dynasimR - Dynamic Agent-Node Simulation Analysis
text: domain-neutral analysis visualisation layer discrete-event, agent-based node-actor simulation outputs. package schema-harmonised two output profiles (Profile Profile B) can analysed single API. Typical use cases include system entities (actors, nodes, agents) move states interact within spatial network environments — example flow analyses, state-transition studies, resource allocation policy comparisons.

title: dynasimR - Dynamic Agent-Node Simulation Analysis
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("rabanheller/dynasimR")

title: dynasimR - Dynamic Agent-Node Simulation Analysis
code: library(dynasimR)  # 1. Load bundled example data (or point to your own simulation outputs) sim <- load_example_data() # sim <- read_simulation("~/my-simulation/data/raw/")  # 2. Time-to-event analysis km <- km_estimate(sim, endpoint = "stage2", stratify_by = "scenario") plot_km(km, title = "Time-to-stage-2")  # 3. Policy effect (policy A vs. policy B) pol <- policy_effect(sim, policy_a_scenario = "A-S08",                      policy_b_scenario = "A-S07") cat(pol$narrative)   # ready to drop into a report  # 4. Autonomy-level trade-off al <- al_efficiency(sim) plot_al_tradeoff(al)  # 5. Manuscript export export_latex_table(   data     = pol$effect_sizes,   filename = "policy_table.tex",   caption  = "Policy effect sizes.",   label    = "policy" )  # 6. Interactive Shiny dashboard launch_app()

title: dynasimR - Dynamic Agent-Node Simulation Analysis
text: R >= 4.1.0 (base pipe |> used throughout) Required imports: dplyr, tidyr, purrr, readr, tibble, ggplot2, survival, rlang, cli, glue Shiny app requires Suggests: dependencies — install dynasimR::check_app_dependencies()

title: dynasimR - Dynamic Agent-Node Simulation Analysis
text: MIT (c) R. Heller

what: Compute AL-Efficiency ratio (survival advantage vs. IHL compliance) — al_efficiency
title: Compute AL-Efficiency ratio (survival advantage vs. IHL compliance) — al_efficiency
text: Quantifies trade-KIA-rate reduction IHL compliance across UAV autonomy levels AL0-AL5. Returns trade-table, optimal AL level (highest KIA reduction still IHL threshold), IHL violations detection-bias summary available.

title: Compute AL-Efficiency ratio (survival advantage vs. IHL compliance) — al_efficiency
code: al_efficiency(   data,   al_scenarios = c(`0` = "M-S00", `1` = "M-S05", `2` = "M-S09", `3` = "M-S10", `4` =     "M-S11", `5` = "M-S12"),   ihl_threshold = 0.8,   reference_al = 0,   n_bootstrap = 1000 )

title: Compute AL-Efficiency ratio (survival advantage vs. IHL compliance) — al_efficiency
text: data dynasimR_data object summary tibble. al_scenarios Named character vector. Mapping AL level (string) scenario ID. Default assumes MEDTACS scenarios ("M-" prefix). ihl_threshold Numeric. Minimum IHL compliance index AL level considered "ethically acceptable". Default 0.80. reference_al Integer. AL level used reference delta calculation. Default 0. n_bootstrap Integer. Bootstrap replicates CIs. Default 1000.

title: Compute AL-Efficiency ratio (survival advantage vs. IHL compliance) — al_efficiency
text: S3 object class dynasimR_al_analysis slots tradeoff_table, optimal_al, ihl_violations params.

title: Compute AL-Efficiency ratio (survival advantage vs. IHL compliance) — al_efficiency
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ sim <- load_example_data() al  <- al_efficiency(sim) plot_al_tradeoff(al) } # }

what: Check availability of Shiny-app dependencies — check_app_dependencies
title: Check availability of Shiny-app dependencies — check_app_dependencies
text: Inspects Suggests: packages required embedded Shiny dashboard prints summary ones installed.

title: Check availability of Shiny-app dependencies — check_app_dependencies
code: check_app_dependencies()

title: Check availability of Shiny-app dependencies — check_app_dependencies
text: tibble columns package, installed, version, invisibly.

title: IHL-Compliance Index — compute_ihl_index
text: Fraction casualties (irrespective identity) receive first care within defined time window post-injury. value 0.80 treated critical IHL violation MEDTACS-SIM ethics analysis.

title: IHL-Compliance Index — compute_ihl_index
code: compute_ihl_index(   casualties,   window_min = 60,   window_unit = c("min", "hours", "days"),   by_scenario = TRUE,   by_identity = TRUE,   n_bootstrap = 1000 )

title: IHL-Compliance Index — compute_ihl_index
text: casualties dynasimR_data object, casualty-level tibble/data.frame time_to_first_care column (MEDTACS) waiting_days_to_min column (REHASIM). window_min Numeric. Window size, expressed unit given window_unit. Default 60. window_unit Character. One "min", "hours", "days". Values internally normalised minutes. by_scenario Logical. Compute per scenario. Default TRUE. by_identity Logical. Stratify identity column present. Default TRUE. n_bootstrap Integer. Bootstrap replicates CIs. Default 1000.

title: IHL-Compliance Index — compute_ihl_index
text: tibble columns scenario (stratified), identity (stratified), ici, ici_ci_lo, ici_ci_hi, n_total, n_treated_in_window, ihl_critical, window_min.

title: IHL-Compliance Index — compute_ihl_index
text: REHASIM data analogous metric uses waiting_days 14-day window (window_unit = "days").

title: IHL-Compliance Index — compute_ihl_index
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ sim <- load_example_data() ihl <- compute_ihl_index(sim) } # }

title: Return-to-Duty outcome analysis (multinomial) — compute_rtd_analysis
text: Fits multinomial logistic regression RTD outcome (categorical) scenario + covariates. Returns model, tidied coefficients, class-level proportions.

title: Return-to-Duty outcome analysis (multinomial) — compute_rtd_analysis
code: compute_rtd_analysis(data, covariates = c("scenario", "injury_severity"))

title: Return-to-Duty outcome analysis (multinomial) — compute_rtd_analysis
text: data dynasimR_data object casualty tibble rtd_outcome column (factor). covariates Character vector. Additional predictors. Default c("scenario", "injury_severity").

title: Return-to-Duty outcome analysis (multinomial) — compute_rtd_analysis
text: S3 object class dynasimR_rtd slots fit, tidy, proportions, params.

title: Waiting-Gap Index (REHASIM) — compute_waiting_gap_index
text: Analogue IHL-Compliance Index rehabilitation flow: fraction casualties wait longer threshold number days (default 14) injury rehabilitation start. value 0.80 flagged policy-relevant violation.

title: Waiting-Gap Index (REHASIM) — compute_waiting_gap_index
code: compute_waiting_gap_index(   data,   window_days = 14,   by_scenario = TRUE,   by_cohort = TRUE,   n_bootstrap = 1000 )

title: Waiting-Gap Index (REHASIM) — compute_waiting_gap_index
text: data dynasimR_data object casualty tibble waiting_days column (waiting_days_to_min minutes). window_days Numeric. Allowed waiting time days. Default 14. by_scenario Logical. Stratify scenario. Default TRUE. by_cohort Logical. Stratify cohort (present). Default TRUE. n_bootstrap Integer. Bootstrap replicates CIs. Default 1000.

title: Waiting-Gap Index (REHASIM) — compute_waiting_gap_index
text: tibble columns scenario (stratified), cohort (stratified), wgi, wgi_ci_lo, wgi_ci_hi, n_total, n_in_window, wgi_critical, window_days.

what: Cox proportional-hazards model for simulation data — cox_model
title: Cox proportional-hazards model for simulation data — cox_model
text: Cox proportional-hazards model simulation data

title: Cox proportional-hazards model for simulation data — cox_model
code: cox_model(   data,   endpoint = c("role2", "overall", "ttd"),   covariates = c("scenario", "injury_severity", "identity"),   reference_scenario = "M-S00" )

title: Cox proportional-hazards model for simulation data — cox_model
text: data dynasimR_data object casualty tibble. endpoint See km_estimate(). covariates Character vector. Covariates entering Cox model. Default c("scenario","injury_severity","identity"). reference_scenario Character. Reference level scenario factor. Default "M-S00". Use "R-S00" REHASIM.

title: Cox proportional-hazards model for simulation data — cox_model
text: S3 object class dynasimR_cox slots fit, tidy, ph_test, forest_data params.

what: Bottleneck detection across roles of care — detect_bottlenecks
title: Bottleneck detection across roles of care — detect_bottlenecks
text: Identifies scenario-role combinations median transit time exceeds percentile threshold relative grand distribution.

title: Bottleneck detection across roles of care — detect_bottlenecks
code: detect_bottlenecks(data, threshold = 0.75)

title: Bottleneck detection across roles of care — detect_bottlenecks
text: data dynasimR_data object throughput tibble role_throughput(). threshold Numeric. Quantile role flagged bottleneck (0-1). Default 0.75 (top quartile).

title: Bottleneck detection across roles of care — detect_bottlenecks
text: tibble bottleneck rows.

what: Quantify doctrine effect (MUF vs. Military Necessity) — doctrine_effect
title: Quantify doctrine effect (MUF vs. Military Necessity) — doctrine_effect
text: Computes full suite effect metrics comparing two scenarios differ medical doctrine (Medical Urgency First vs. Military Necessity). Returns deltas KIA rate IHL-Compliance Index, Cohen's d, risk differences auto-generated narrative sentence suitable pasting manuscript.

title: Quantify doctrine effect (MUF vs. Military Necessity) — doctrine_effect
code: doctrine_effect(   data,   muf_scenario,   milnec_scenario,   alpha = 0.05,   n_bootstrap = 1000 )

title: Quantify doctrine effect (MUF vs. Military Necessity) — doctrine_effect
text: data dynasimR_data object summary tibble. muf_scenario Character. Scenario ID using MUF doctrine. milnec_scenario Character. Scenario ID using Military Necessity doctrine. alpha Numeric. Significance level. Default 0.05. n_bootstrap Integer. Bootstrap replicates CIs. Default 1000.

title: Quantify doctrine effect (MUF vs. Military Necessity) — doctrine_effect
text: S3 object class dynasimR_doctrine slots delta_kia, ihl_comparison, effect_sizes, wilcoxon_tests, narrative, params.

title: Quantify doctrine effect (MUF vs. Military Necessity) — doctrine_effect
text: hardcoded defaults used — must supply scenario IDs explicitly, function works MEDTACS (e.g. "M-S08" vs. "M-S07") REHASIM (e.g. "R-S19" vs. "R-S00").

title: Quantify doctrine effect (MUF vs. Military Necessity) — doctrine_effect
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ sim <- load_example_data() doc <- doctrine_effect(sim,   muf_scenario = "M-S08", milnec_scenario = "M-S07") print(doc) cat(doc$narrative) } # }

what: dynasimR: Dynamic Agent-Node Simulation Analysis — dynasimR-package
title: dynasimR: Dynamic Agent-Node Simulation Analysis — dynasimR-package
text: Analysis visualisation layer discrete-event, agent-based node-actor simulation outputs. Primary application MEDTACS-SIM military medical simulation (chain resuscitation, doctrine effects, autonomy trade-offs) REHASIM rehabilitation flow simulation (FIM trajectories, waiting-gap index, supply-demand).

title: dynasimR: Dynamic Agent-Node Simulation Analysis — dynasimR-package
text: package schema-harmonised simulations can analysed API. See vignettes worked examples.

title: dynasimR: Dynamic Agent-Node Simulation Analysis — dynasimR-package
text: Data /O read_simulation(), load_example_data(), validate_dynasimR_data() Survival km_estimate(), cox_model() Doctrine doctrine_effect() Autonomy al_efficiency() IHL compute_ihl_index() Patient flow role_throughput(), detect_bottlenecks() UAV uav_comparison() Sensitivity morris_screening(), tornado_data() REHASIM fim_trajectory_analysis(), compute_waiting_gap_index(), mil_civil_effect(), spatial_supply_demand(), compute_rtd_analysis() Plots plot_km(), plot_forest(), plot_al_tradeoff(), plot_scenario_heatmap(), plot_doctrine(), plot_timeline(), plot_map(), plot_fim_curves(), plot_sdi_map(), plot_cost_effectiveness() Export export_figure(), export_latex_table(), fill_placeholders() Shiny launch_app(), check_app_dependencies()

what: Export a ggplot in publication-ready format — export_figure
title: Export a ggplot in publication-ready format — export_figure
text: Writes ggplot2 object PDF/SVG/PNG/TIFF/EPS specified physical dimensions (millimetres) resolution.

title: Export a ggplot in publication-ready format — export_figure
code: export_figure(plot, filename, width_mm = 174, height_mm = 120, dpi = 300)

title: Export a ggplot in publication-ready format — export_figure
text: plot ggplot2 object. filename Character. Output path (extension determines format). width_mm Numeric. Width mm. 174 = Springer single-column, 88 = double-column. Default 174. height_mm Numeric. Height mm. Default 120. dpi Integer. Raster resolution. Default 300.

title: Export a ggplot in publication-ready format — export_figure
text: Invisibly, filename.

what: Export a tibble as a Springer Nature compatible LaTeX table — export_latex_table
title: Export a tibble as a Springer Nature compatible LaTeX table — export_latex_table
text: Produces booktabs-style table sn-jnl.cls requirement using \\botrule instead \\bottomrule escaping </> cells. Optionally appends tablenotes footnote.

title: Export a tibble as a Springer Nature compatible LaTeX table — export_latex_table
code: export_latex_table(   data,   filename,   caption,   label,   note = NULL,   digits = 3,   col_format = NULL )

title: Export a tibble as a Springer Nature compatible LaTeX table — export_latex_table
text: data tibble/data.frame. filename Character. Output .tex path. caption Character. Table caption. label Character. LaTeX label (without tab: prefix; added). note Character. Optional footnote text. digits Integer. Digits numeric columns. Default 3. col_format Character vector. Column alignment spec (e.g. c("l", "r", "r")). Default NULL picks "l" first column "r" rest.

title: Export a tibble as a Springer Nature compatible LaTeX table — export_latex_table
text: LaTeX source, invisibly.

title: Export a tibble as a Springer Nature compatible LaTeX table — export_latex_table
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ export_latex_table(head(iris), "iris.tex",                    caption = "First rows of iris.",                    label   = "iris") } # }

what: Fill [XX_*] placeholders in a LaTeX manuscript — fill_placeholders
title: Fill [XX_*] placeholders in a LaTeX manuscript — fill_placeholders
text: Substitutes [XX_*] placeholders LaTeX source file automatically computed simulation statistics. mapping placeholder computation assembled inside function doctrine_effect(), al_efficiency() compute_ihl_index().

title: Fill [XX_*] placeholders in a LaTeX manuscript — fill_placeholders
code: fill_placeholders(   sim_data,   tex_file,   output_file = NULL,   dry_run = FALSE,   muf_scenario = "M-S08",   milnec_scenario = "M-S07",   baseline_scenario = "M-S00" )

title: Fill [XX_*] placeholders in a LaTeX manuscript — fill_placeholders
text: sim_data dynasimR_data object. tex_file Character. Path LaTeX source file. output_file Character. Output path; default appends _filled .tex. dry_run Logical. TRUE, print replacements write file. Default FALSE. muf_scenario Character. MUF scenario ID. Default "M-S08". milnec_scenario Character. MilNec scenario ID. Default "M-S07". baseline_scenario Character. Baseline scenario ID. Default "M-S00".

title: Fill [XX_*] placeholders in a LaTeX manuscript — fill_placeholders
text: Named character vector replacements (invisibly).

title: FIM trajectory analysis (REHASIM) — fim_trajectory_analysis
text: Summarises longitudinal Functional Independence Measure (FIM) trajectories scenario (optionally) cohort. Returns per-replication summary (admission, discharge, gain) longitudinal tidy table suitable plot_fim_curves().

title: FIM trajectory analysis (REHASIM) — fim_trajectory_analysis
code: fim_trajectory_analysis(data, group_by = c("scenario"), longitudinal = FALSE)

title: FIM trajectory analysis (REHASIM) — fim_trajectory_analysis
text: data dynasimR_data object casualty/summary tibble contains least fim_admission fim_discharge. group_by Character. Grouping columns. Default c("scenario"). longitudinal Logical. TRUE, returns per-timepoint trajectories fim_timeseries slot present data.

title: FIM trajectory analysis (REHASIM) — fim_trajectory_analysis
text: S3 object class dynasimR_fim slots summary, longitudinal (available) params.

what: Kaplan-Meier estimator for simulation data — km_estimate
title: Kaplan-Meier estimator for simulation data — km_estimate
text: Estimates survival functions simulated casualty outcomes. Supports three endpoints (survival--Role-2, overall survival, time--first-care) arbitrary stratification.

title: Kaplan-Meier estimator for simulation data — km_estimate
code: km_estimate(   data,   scenarios = NULL,   endpoint = c("role2", "overall", "ttd", "ais_conversion", "discharge", "phase_c"),   stratify_by = "scenario",   ci_method = "log",   n_bootstrap = 0,   seed = 42 )

title: Kaplan-Meier estimator for simulation data — km_estimate
text: data dynasimR_data object tibble/data.frame containing casualty-level columns. scenarios Character vector. Restrict scenario IDs. Default NULL = . endpoint Character. One "role2", "overall", "ttd", "ais_conversion", "discharge", "phase_c". latter three REHASIM-. stratify_by Character vector. Strata variables, e.g. "scenario" c("scenario", "identity"). ci_method Character. Passed survival::survfit() conf.type. Default "log". n_bootstrap Integer. Bootstrap replicates KM CIs (0 = disabled). Default 0. seed Integer. RNG seed bootstrap. Default 42.

title: Kaplan-Meier estimator for simulation data — km_estimate
text: S3 object class dynasimR_km (list) slots fit, tidy, logrank, median_survival, boot_ci params.

title: Kaplan-Meier estimator for simulation data — km_estimate
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ sim <- load_example_data() km <- km_estimate(sim, endpoint = "role2", stratify_by = "scenario") print(km) plot_km(km) } # }

what: Military vs. civilian prioritisation effect (REHASIM) — mil_civil_effect

title: Military vs. civilian prioritisation effect (REHASIM) — mil_civil_effect
text: data dynasimR_data object summary tibble. mil_scenario Character. Scenario ID military priority. civ_scenario Character. Scenario ID civilian-inclusive priority. outcome_col Character. Column name RTD outcome. Default "rtd_rate". cost_col Character. Column name cost metric. Default "cost". alpha Numeric. Significance level. Default 0.05. n_bootstrap Integer. Bootstrap replicates. Default 1000.

title: Military vs. civilian prioritisation effect (REHASIM) — mil_civil_effect
text: S3 object class dynasimR_mil_civil slots rtd, cost, delta_rtd, delta_cost, params.

title: Morris-style sensitivity screening from simulation replicates — morris_screening
text: tibble columns input, mu_star (mean absolute change), sigma, rank.

text: data dynasimR_data object summary tibble. reference Character. Scenario ID treated comparator. scenarios Character vector. Scenarios evaluate reference. outcome_col Character. Outcome column. Default "rtd_rate". cost_col Character. Cost column. Default "cost". wtp Numeric vector. Willingness--pay thresholds CEAC. Default seq(0, 2e5, length.= 40). show_ceac Logical. TRUE, return CEAC plot instead CE plane. Default FALSE.

text: Plots dynasimR_km object. Use options control confidence-interval ribbons, log-rank p-value annotation colour grouping.

title: Kaplan-Meier survival curves — plot_km
code: plot_km(   km_result,   show_ci = TRUE,   show_risktable = FALSE,   show_pval = TRUE,   show_median = TRUE,   color_by = "scenario",   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   xlab = "Time post-injury [min]",   ylab = "Survival probability",   xlim = NULL,   manuscript_width = 174,   ... )

title: Kaplan-Meier survival curves — plot_km
text: km_result dynasimR_km object. show_ci Logical. Show CI ribbon. Default TRUE. show_risktable Logical. Show risk table (requires survminer). Default FALSE (simpler output without survminer). show_pval Logical. Annotate log-rank p-value. Default TRUE. show_median Logical. Draw horizontal 50% reference line. Default TRUE. color_by Character. Aesthetic grouping. One "scenario", "identity", "doctrine". Default "scenario". title, subtitle, xlab, ylab Character overrides. xlim Numeric length-2. Restrict x range. Default NULL. manuscript_width Numeric. mm width hint (directly used , kept API symmetry). Default 174. ... Unused.

title: Kaplan-Meier survival curves — plot_km
text: ggplot2 object.

text: data_dir Character. Path directory containing simulation outputs. Files expected follow pattern {scenario_id}_summary.csv, {scenario_id}_casualties.csv (optionally) {scenario_id}_timeseries.csv. scenarios Character vector. Scenario IDs load. Default NULL loads every scenario found data_dir. validate Logical. Run validate_dynasimR_data() loaded tables. Default TRUE. verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Default TRUE.

code: scenario_meta

title: Scenario metadata for MEDTACS-SIM and REHASIM — scenario_meta
text: tibble 35 rows following columns: id Character. Scenario identifier prefix ("M-" MEDTACS, "R-" REHASIM). simulation Character. "MEDTACS" "REHASIM". label Character. Human-readable scenario name. rq Character. Associated research question ("RQ1".."RQ4", "SENS", "PIPE"). doctrine Character. Doctrine principle ("MUF", "MIL_NEC", "MIXED", "NA"). uav Character. UAV configuration ("NONE", "ISR", "CASEVAC", "ARMED", "MIXED"). al Integer. Autonomy level (0-5) NA.

title: Supply-Demand Index (SDI) per region (REHASIM) — spatial_supply_demand
text: Computes per-region Supply-Demand Index: ratio available rehabilitation capacity demand. Values > 1 indicate oversupply, values < 1 undersupply.

title: Supply-Demand Index (SDI) per region (REHASIM) — spatial_supply_demand

title: Supply-Demand Index (SDI) per region (REHASIM) — spatial_supply_demand
text: data dynasimR_data object summary tibble region, supply demand columns. region_col Character. Region column name. Default "region". supply_col Character. Supply column name. Default "supply". demand_col Character. Demand column name. Default "demand". eps Numeric. Offset avoid division zero. Default 1.

title: Supply-Demand Index (SDI) per region (REHASIM) — spatial_supply_demand
text: tibble columns region, supply_median, demand_median, sdi_median, sdi_q025, sdi_q975, undersupply.

title: dynasimR 0.1.0
text: Initial release.

title: dynasimR 0.1.0
text: Load validate simulation outputs two interchangeable profiles via read_simulation() / load_example_data(). Time--event analysis km_estimate(), cox_model(), plot_km(), plot_forest(). Policy-effect quantification via policy_effect() auto-generated LaTeX-ready narrative. Autonomy-level trade-analysis via al_efficiency() plot_al_tradeoff(). Compliance Index via compute_compliance_index() bootstrap confidence intervals. Profile B helpers: progress_trajectory(), compute_wait_gap_index(), group_effect(), spatial_supply_demand(), compute_completion_analysis(). Manuscript export: export_figure(), export_latex_table(), fill_placeholders() (publisher-template compatible). Embedded Shiny dashboard via launch_app(). Example data shipped reproducible demos tests.
